title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 7 (Fragment 2)
text:
“We’re at war already,” said Old Niccolo. “Go away.”

“I regret,” said the Chief of Police, “that that is impossible.”

“Then don’t say you weren’t warned,” said Guido.

There was a short burst of song from the adults of both families. Paolo wished he knew that spell. It sounded useful. As soon as it was over, Rinaldo and a swarthy young Petrocchi came over to the two policemen and towed them away backwards. They were as stiff as the tailor’s dummies in the barred windows of Grossi’s. Rinaldo and the other young man laid them against the steps of the Art Gallery and returned each to his family, without looking at one another. As for the rest of the Ducal Police, they seemed to have vanished, bicycles and all.

“Ready now?” said Guido.

“Ready,” said Antonio.

And the single combat commenced.

Looking back on it afterwards, Paolo realized that it could not have lasted more than three minutes, though it seemed endless at the time. For, in that time, the strength, skill and speed of both champions was tried to the utmost. The first, and prob ably the longest, part was when the two were testing one another for an opening, and comparatively little seemed to happen. Both stood, leaning slightly forward, muttering, humming, occasionally flicking a hand. Paolo stared at his father’s strained face and wondered just what was going on. Then, momentarily, Guido was a man-shaped red-and-white check duster. Someone gasped. But Antonio almost simultaneously became a cardboard man covered with green triangles. Then both flicked back to themselves again.

The speed of it astounded Paolo. A spell had not only been cast on both sides, but also a counter-spell, and a spell counter to that, all in the time it took someone to gasp. Both combatants were panting and looking warily at each other. It was clear they were very evenly matched.

Again there was a space when nothing seemed to happen, except a sort of flickering on both sides. Then suddenly Antonio struck, and struck so hard that it was plain he had all the time been building a strong spell, beneath the flicker of trivial spells designed to keep Guido occupied. Guido gave a shout and dissolved into dust, which swept away back wards in a spiral. But, somehow, as he dissolved, he threw his strong spell at Antonio. Antonio broke into a thousand little pieces, like a spilled jigsaw puzzle.

For an ageless time, the swirl of dust and the pile of broken Antonio hung in midair. Both were struggling to stay together and not to patter down on the uprooted cobbles of the Corso. In fact, they were still struggling to make spells too. When, at last, Antonio staggered forward in one piece, holding some kind of red fruit in his right hand, he had barely time to dodge. Guido was a leopard in mid-spring.

Elizabeth screamed.

Antonio threw himself to one side, heaved a breath and sang. “Oliphans!” His usually silky voice was rough and ragged, but he hit the right notes. A gigantic elephant, with tusks longer than Paolo was tall, cut off the low sun and shook the Corso as it advanced, ears spread, to trample the attacking leopard. It was hard to believe the great beast was indeed worried, thin Antonio Montana.

For a shadow of a second, the leopard was Guido Petrocchi, very white in the face and luridly red in the beard, gabbling a frantic song. “Hickory-dickory-muggery mus!” And he must have hit the right notes too. He seemed to vanish.

vocabulary.definition: Very surprised or amazed.
vocabulary.example: The speed of it astounded Paolo.

vocabulary.definition: Closed or blocked with bars, often for security.
vocabulary.example: The tailor’s dummies in the barred windows of Grossi’s.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden outbreak or intense effort of something.
vocabulary.example: There was a short burst of song from the adults of both families.

vocabulary.definition: A person who fights or argues for a cause or who has won a contest.
vocabulary.example: The strength, skill and speed of both champions was tried to the utmost.

vocabulary.definition: To begin or start.
vocabulary.example: And the single combat commenced.

vocabulary.definition: A spell cast to negate or reverse the effect of another spell.
vocabulary.example: A spell had not only been cast on both sides, but also a counter-spell.

vocabulary.definition: To disappear or be broken down into smaller parts.
vocabulary.example: Guido gave a shout and dissolved into dust.

vocabulary.definition: Models of human figures used for fitting clothes or display.
vocabulary.example: They were as stiff as the tailor’s dummies in the barred windows of Grossi’s.

vocabulary.definition: Desperate or wild with fear, anxiety, or other emotion.
vocabulary.example: Guido was gabbling a frantic song.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely large or enormous.
vocabulary.example: A gigantic elephant with tusks longer than Paolo was tall.

vocabulary.definition: To breathe heavily or to lift or throw something with effort.
vocabulary.example: Heaved a breath and sang.

vocabulary.definition: To make a low continuous sound with the lips closed.
vocabulary.example: Both stood, leaning slightly forward, muttering, humming.

vocabulary.definition: A large wild cat with a spotted coat.
vocabulary.example: Guido was a leopard in mid-spring.

vocabulary.definition: Speaking quietly and indistinctly.
vocabulary.example: Both stood, leaning slightly forward, muttering, humming.

vocabulary.definition: Breathing quickly and heavily, usually after exertion.
vocabulary.example: Both combatants were panting and looking warily at each other.

vocabulary.definition: Worn out or rough; in voice, sounding harsh or hoarse.
vocabulary.example: His usually silky voice was rough and ragged.

vocabulary.definition: To feel sad or disappointed about something that has happened or been done, especially a loss or missed opportunity.
vocabulary.example: “I regret,” said the Chief of Police, “that that is impossible.”

vocabulary.definition: A word or formula believed to have magical power.
vocabulary.example: Paolo wished he knew that spell.

vocabulary.definition: A shape that winds around a central point, moving outward or inward.
vocabulary.example: Which swept away backwards in a spiral.

vocabulary.definition: To walk or move unsteadily as if about to fall.
vocabulary.example: Antonio staggered forward in one piece.

vocabulary.definition: The quality or state of being strong, power.
vocabulary.example: The strength, skill and speed of both champions was tried to the utmost.

vocabulary.definition: Having a dark complexion or skin tone.
vocabulary.example: Rinaldo and a swarthy young Petrocchi came over to the two policemen.

vocabulary.definition: To examine or evaluate by trial.
vocabulary.example: The two were testing one another for an opening.

vocabulary.definition: To step heavily on something or someone, causing damage.
vocabulary.example: The elephant advanced to trample the attacking leopard.

vocabulary.definition: To disappear suddenly and completely.
vocabulary.example: He seemed to vanish.

vocabulary.definition: To disappear suddenly and completely.
vocabulary.example: They seemed to have vanished, bicycles and all.

vocabulary.definition: Cautiously or carefully, especially to avoid danger or problems.
vocabulary.example: Both combatants were panting and looking warily at each other.
